description:
Epic created from issue github:aronasorman/sling#28

URL: https://github.com/aronasorman/sling/issues/28

## Overview

`sling start` currently requires a GitHub issue ref or Linear ID. This feature adds support for plain text descriptions — when you don't have a ticket yet, just describe what you want and Opus turns it into a structured spec.

```
sling start "add rate limiting to the API endpoints"
```

## Detection Logic

In `issue/source.go`, add a `DescriptionSource` that matches when input does not look like a known ref:
- No `http://` or `https://`
- No `#` character  
- No `LIN-` prefix
- Longer than ~10 chars (avoid accidental matches on typos)

Order of detection: GitHub → Linear → Description (fallback).

## Spec Agent

Spawn an Opus agent in `internal/pipeline/specgen.go` with this system prompt:

```
You are Sling's Spec agent. Turn a raw feature description into a structured engineering spec.

Output a JSON file to the path provided. Schema:
{
  "title": "short imperative title (max 60 chars)",
  "description": "1-2 paragraph explanation of what and why",
  "implementation_notes": "key technical decisions, constraints, patterns to follow",
  "acceptance_criteria": ["list of testable conditions"]
}

Rules:
- Write JSON to the output file. Do not print to stdout.
- Be concrete and implementation-focused.
- Acceptance criteria must be testable (no vague "works correctly").
```

Output path: `/tmp/sling-spec-\<timestamp\>.json`

## Integration

1. `internal/issue/description.go` — `DescriptionSource` that stores the raw input
2. `internal/pipeline/specgen.go` — `RunSpecAgent(desc, repoRoot, contextFiles) (*Issue, error)` — spawns Opus, reads JSON output, returns `*issue.Issue`  
3. `internal/pipeline/intake.go` — before creating the epic bead, if source is `DescriptionSource`, call `RunSpecAgent` first and use its output as the issue

The spec agent output maps directly to `issue.Issue`: title → Title, description + implementation_notes → Body, acceptance_criteria appended to Body.

## Code changes

- `internal/issue/description.go` (new) — `DescriptionSource` implementing `IssueSource`
- `internal/pipeline/specgen.go` (new) — `RunSpecAgent`
- `internal/pipeline/intake.go` — call specgen when source is description
- `internal/agent/agent.go` — add `SpecAgentSystemPrompt(outputPath string)`
- `internal/issue/source.go` — register `DescriptionSource` as fallback in `DetectSource`

## Acceptance criteria

- `sling start "add rate limiting to the API endpoints"` runs without error
- Opus produces a spec with title, description, implementation_notes, acceptance_criteria
- Epic bead is created with the full spec as body
- Planner can read the bead and decompose it into child beads
- `sling start gh#42` still works (regression)
- `sling start LIN-100` still works (regression)
title: feat: plain text description intake with Opus spec agent

description:
## What

Create `internal/issue/description.go` with a `DescriptionSource` struct implementing the `Source` interface.

## Implementation

- `DescriptionSource` stores the raw text description passed by the user.
- `Fetch(ctx, ref)` returns an `*Issue` with:
  - `ID`: `"description:<first-8-chars-of-sha256>"`
  - `Title`: truncated first line of description (max 60 chars)
  - `Body`: the full raw description text
  - `URL`: empty string
- Add a helper `IsDescriptionSource(src Source) bool` that type-asserts to `*DescriptionSource` (needed later by intake).
- Constructor: `NewDescription(rawText string) *DescriptionSource`.

## Testing

- Unit test in `internal/issue/description_test.go`:
  - `Fetch` returns an Issue with correct ID format, title truncation, and full body.
  - `IsDescriptionSource` returns true for `*DescriptionSource`, false for `*GitHub`.
  - Empty or whitespace-only input returns an error from `Fetch`.
title: Add DescriptionSource type in issue package

description:
## What

Update `DetectSource` in `internal/issue/issue.go` to return a `DescriptionSource` when the input doesn't match any known ref format.

## Implementation

Add detection logic in `DetectSource` (after GitHub and Linear checks) for the `"auto"` configured source:

```
if !strings.HasPrefix(ref, "http://") && !strings.HasPrefix(ref, "https://") &&
   !strings.Contains(ref, "#") && !looksLikeLinear(ref) && len(ref) > 10 {
    return NewDescription(ref), nil
}
```

Also support `configured == "description"` to force description mode.

Detection order remains: explicit config → Linear heuristic → GitHub (default) but now with description as a final fallback before the GitHub default when the ref is clearly not a ticket reference.

## Testing

- Unit tests in `internal/issue/issue_test.go` (or a new `detect_test.go`):
  - `DetectSource("auto", "add rate limiting to API endpoints", ...)` → returns `*DescriptionSource`
  - `DetectSource("auto", "42", ...)` → returns `*GitHub` (short, looks like issue number)
  - `DetectSource("auto", "ENG-123", ...)` → returns `*Linear`
  - `DetectSource("auto", "https://github.com/...", ...)` → does NOT return DescriptionSource
  - `DetectSource("auto", "owner/repo#42", ...)` → does NOT return DescriptionSource (has `#`)
  - `DetectSource("description", "anything", ...)` → returns `*DescriptionSource`
title: Register DescriptionSource as fallback in DetectSource

description:
## What

Add the Opus spec agent that converts raw descriptions into structured engineering specs.

## Implementation

### agent/agent.go

Add `RoleSpec Role = "spec"` constant.

Add `SpecAgentSystemPrompt(outputPath string) string` that returns the system prompt from the epic description:
- Instructs the agent to output JSON to the given path
- Schema: `{ title, description, implementation_notes, acceptance_criteria }`
- Rules: write to file not stdout, be concrete, testable acceptance criteria

### pipeline/specgen.go (new)

Add `RunSpecAgent(ctx context.Context, description, repoRoot string, contextFiles map[string]string) (*issue.Issue, error)`:

1. Generate output path: `/tmp/sling-spec-<unix-timestamp>.json`
2. Build system prompt via `agent.SpecAgentSystemPrompt(outputPath)`
3. Build user prompt: the raw description + any context file summaries
4. Call `agent.Run(ctx, agent.RunOptions{...})` with model `claude-opus-4-6`, MaxTurns ~15
5. Read and parse the JSON output file
6. Map to `*issue.Issue`:
   - `Title` → spec title
   - `Body` → description + `\n\n## Implementation Notes\n` + implementation_notes + `\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n` + bullet list of acceptance_criteria
   - `ID` → `"spec:<first-8-of-sha256-of-description>"`
   - `URL` → empty
7. Clean up the temp file
8. Handle errors: file not found, invalid JSON, missing required fields

Add a `specOutput` struct for JSON deserialization.

## Testing

- Unit test for `SpecAgentSystemPrompt`: verify it contains the output path and required schema fields.
- Unit test for the JSON parsing logic: given a valid spec JSON file on disk, `parseSpecOutput(path)` returns correct `*issue.Issue`. Test with missing fields, malformed JSON.
- Integration test for `RunSpecAgent` can be skipped (requires live Opus) — but add a `TestParseSpecOutput` that exercises the file-reading and mapping logic.
title: Add SpecAgent system prompt and RunSpecAgent pipeline function

description:
## What

Wire everything together so `sling start "plain text"` detects a description, runs the spec agent, and creates an epic bead with the structured spec.

## Implementation

### pipeline/intake.go

Modify `Intake` signature or add a new `IntakeWithSpec` variant:

```go
func Intake(ctx context.Context, ref string, src issue.Source, repoRoot string, contextFiles map[string]string) (*IntakeResult, error)
```

- After fetching the issue from `src`, check `issue.IsDescriptionSource(src)`.
- If true, call `RunSpecAgent(ctx, fetchedIssue.Body, repoRoot, contextFiles)` to get the enriched `*issue.Issue`.
- Use the spec agent's output (structured title + body) for the epic bead creation instead of the raw description.
- If spec agent fails, fall back to using the raw description with a warning log.

### cmd/sling/start.go

- Update the `start` command to pass `repoRoot` and `contextFiles` through to `Intake`.
- These are already loaded in start.go for the planner — just pass them earlier.
- Ensure the CLI arg parsing accepts a bare string (not just issue refs). The cobra command likely already accepts a positional arg — verify it works with quoted multi-word strings.

## Testing

- **Unit test**: Mock or stub `RunSpecAgent` to verify intake calls it when source is `DescriptionSource` and skips it for `GitHub`/`Linear`.
- **Integration smoke test** (manual): `sling start "add rate limiting to the API endpoints"` should:
  1. Detect as DescriptionSource
  2. Spawn Opus spec agent
  3. Create epic bead with structured spec
  4. Proceed to planning
- **Regression**: `sling start` with a GitHub ref (e.g., `gh#42`) must still work — the DescriptionSource path is not triggered.
- **Regression**: `sling start` with a Linear ref must still work.
title: Integrate spec agent into intake pipeline and CLI

description:
Epic created from issue github:aronasorman/sling#33

URL: https://github.com/aronasorman/sling/issues/33

## Current model (wrong)

- One jj worktree per sub-bead
- One agent per sub-bead  
- One branch per sub-bead
- Per-bead automated review loop

## New model

**One epic = one worktree = one agent = one branch = one PR**

- `sling next` claims an epic bead, creates one jj worktree for it
- One Sonnet agent runs in that worktree for the full epic
- Agent works through sub-beads sequentially, respecting DAG order
- Each sub-bead becomes a commit in the worktree (`jj commit -m "bead: <title>"`)
- Sub-beads tracked in bd for progress visibility, but no per-sub-bead worktree/branch/agent
- When all sub-beads done: `sling signal-done <epic-id>`
- Epic-level review: build+test gate, then one Sonnet holistic review pass on full diff
- `sling done <epic-id>`: push branch, open PR, human reviews once

## Changes needed

### internal/pipeline/execute.go
- `Execute()` takes the epic bead ID, creates one worktree for it
- System prompt includes all sub-beads as a sequenced task list
- Agent commits each sub-bead as it completes
- Remove per-sub-bead claim/label transitions during execution (just track in bd as metadata)
- After agent exits (signal-done): run build+test, then one automated review pass on full epic diff

### internal/pipeline/expand.go
- Sub-beads still created in bd for tracking, but no `sling:ready` label promotion per-bead
- DAG order communicated to the executor agent in its system prompt, not enforced via bd labels

### internal/agent/agent.go — ExecutorSystemPrompt
- Include ordered sub-bead list with titles + descriptions
- Instruct agent to commit after each sub-bead: `jj commit -m "bead: <title>"`
- Final step: `sling signal-done <epic-id>`

### Review (internal/pipeline/review.go)
- Run once after epic signal-done
- Build+test gate first (fast fail)
- One Sonnet review pass on full epic diff (not per-bead)
- One addresser pass
- Then label epic `sling:review-pending`

### Escalation (new)
- If agent fails 2x: label epic `sling:escalated`, notify human
- If any file in security-sensitive paths touched: notify human immediately after review

## What stays the same
- `sling start` (intake + planning)  
- `sling done <epic-id>` (squash + push + PR)
- `sling signal-done` command
- bd for progress tracking
- jj for VCS
title: redesign: one worktree per epic, sequential sub-bead execution, one PR
